text: The African Independent Churches Association (AICA) constitutes a new development in South African theological education. The AICA decided on their own Theological College initially, but later decided on a partnership with the Umphumulo Lutheran Theological College (ULTC) for training students at the college. This decision by the AICs to train their pastors through the ULTC, was driven by a mission to ensure that pastoral education strengthens the doctrine of the Church, while it is contemporary and relevant to the experiences of the people in the Church. They regard pastoral education as a service to the community. This article will explore the reason for the partnerships between the AICs and the mainstream South African theological education institutions, the experiences of the AICA Theological College, and will finally conclude with the reasons for the curriculum problems experienced at the college, and the remedies provided by the Lutheran church.
explanation: The text has a formal, generic academic tone with minor inconsistencies (AICA vs AICs) and slightly awkward phrasing, which can be characteristic of AI-generated content, though it could also be human-written.
